hooks.prompt: Review the i18n/localization code change for issues. Check for: 1) Hardcoded user-visible string not wrapped in translation function (missed i18n — all user-facing text must use t(), formatMessage(), or equivalent translation wrapper), 2) String concatenation for building translated sentences (word order differs between languages — use ICU MessageFormat with placeholders: '{count} items' not count + ' items'), 3) Date, number, or currency formatted without locale parameter (displays in server locale, not user locale — use Intl.DateTimeFormat, Intl.NumberFormat with user's locale), 4) Pluralization using simple if/else instead of ICU plural rules (English has 2 forms but Arabic has 6, Polish has 4 — use {count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}} format), 5) Translation keys that are full English sentences instead of semantic keys (fragile, hard to maintain — use 'user.profile.title' not 'Welcome to your profile'), 6) Missing lang attribute on HTML element or missing dir attribute for RTL languages (accessibility and rendering issues — set html lang and dir attributes from user locale), 7) Right-to-left (RTL) layout not supported (CSS layout breaks for Arabic, Hebrew — use logical CSS properties: margin-inline-start not margin-left, and test with dir="rtl"), 8) Translation file with missing keys compared to default locale (untranslated strings show keys or fallback silently — lint translation files for completeness against source locale), 9) Locale detection only from browser language without user preference override or fallback chain (user may prefer different locale — support preference > URL param > cookie > Accept-Language > default), 10) Images, icons, or media with embedded text that cannot be translated (hardcoded text in images — use overlaid text elements or locale-specific image variants). Return {"ok": true} if no issues, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue found"} if problems detected.
